title: In-silico protocols for reproducibility of machine learning approaches
author: Solanki, Dhwani
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This poster shows our in-silico protocols approach to improve  reproducibility of Machine Learning (ML). It builds on top of  SMART Protocols  for wet-lab experiments to adapt them and extend them to cover Machine Learning experiments. 
 Lab-protocols often accompany wet-lab experiments as text-based documents describing the sequence of tasks and operations executed, including, e.g., references to equipment, reagents troubleshooting and tips.  SMART Protocols  provides a semantic layer for lab-protocols so, for instance, reagents are linked to (semantic) chemical databases such as ChEMBL. 
 Similarly to wet-lab experiments, ML experiments are also composed of inputs, steps and outputs and would benefit from semantically enriched in-silico protocols. We aim at providing a semantic layer, namely in-silico protocol, for ML experiments supporting FAIRness for ML, transparency and reproducible outcomes. 
 More information can be found at  GitHub Repository . 
  Funding  
 This work is partially funded by the  German Research Foundation (DFG)  under the  grant No. 460234259  corresponding to  NFDI4DataScience consortium .
